content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Peter A. Bagatelos 
Bagatelos & Fadem 
August I, 1989 
The International Building 
601 California street, suite 1801 
San Francisco, CA 
94108 
Dear Mr. Bagatelos: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-395 
This is in response to your request for confirmation of 
telephone advice regarding the status of Regulation 18536.2 of the 
Political Reform Act (the "Act") .1/ 
This letter confirms that your letter dated June 30, 1989 ac-
curately summarizes the telephone advice I provided to you on June 
14, 1989, concerning the emergency amendments to Regulation 
18536.2. 
As I stated in our telephone conversation, on May 2, 
1989, the Commission adopted emergency amendments to Regulation 
18536.2 which substantially changed the scope of the regulation. 
However, the emergency amendment of Regulation 18536.2 was 
rejected by the Office of Administrative Law on May 22, 1989. 
At 
that time, the Commission decided to appeal the decision and 
advise pursuant to the emergency regulation as adopted by the 
Commission on May 2, 1989. 
On July 7, 1989, the Governor rejected the Commission's ap-
peal of OAL's decision. 
Consequently, we are no longer advising 
pursuant to the emergency amendments to Regulation 18536.2. 
A 
copy of the regulation as now enforced is enclosed. 
The 
Commission is considering its options in response to the 
Governor's decision. 
In addition, please note that on May 15, 1989, in Service 
Employees International Union, AFL-CIO, et ale v. Fair Political 
1/ 
Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory refer-
ences are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Com-
mission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
section 18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to 
Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660 


File No. A-89-395 
Page 2 
Practices Commission, united States District Court, Eastern 
District of California, Case No. CIVS-89-0433, LKK-JFM, the 
District Court granted plaintiff's motion for a preliminary 
injunction to enjoin enforcement of Section 85306 in regard to 
contributions collected prior to January 1, 1989 which are in 
compliance with the contribution limits of the Act. 2 / 
On August 
11, 1989 the court will hear a summary judgment motion challenging 
the constitutionality of Section 85306. 
If you have any further questions regarding this matter, 
please feel free to contact this office at (916) 322-5901. 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
/G\a,l co:n~:~ , , , __ 
/ By: 
~ohn W. Wallace 
~~ Counsel, Legal Division 
KED:JWW:plh 
Enclosure 
2/ 
section 85306 states: 
"Any person who possesses campaign 
funds on the effective date of this chapter may expend these funds 
for any lawful purpose other than to support or oppose a candidacy 
for elective office." 
section 85306 serves as the statutory basis 
for Regulation 18536.2. 


BARRY FADEM 
PETER A. BAGATELOS 
WES VAN WINKLE 
Mr. John Wallace 
Legal Division 
LAW OFFICES OF 
BAGATELOS & FADEM 
THE INTERNATIONAL BUILDING 
601 CALIFORNIA STREET 
SUITE 1801 
SAN FRANCISCO. CALIFORNIA 94108 
June 30, 1989 
Fair Political Practices 
Commission 
428 "J" street 
Suite 800 
P.O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 
Dear Mr. Wallace: 
TELEPHONE 
(4IS) 982-7100 
FAX 
(415) 982-108S 
This will confirm our telephone conversation on June 14, 
1989. 
I had called and asked regarding the status of regulation 
18536.2 and the ability of committees to use restricted funds 
for any lawful purpose. 
You advised me that the Office of Administrative Law (OAL) 
had rejected the Commission's action on an emergency basis in 
rescinding portions of that regulation. 
Therefore, regulation 
18536.2 is still in effect. 
However, you further advised me that the commission staff is 
still following the emergency approach taken by the Commission, 
even though the OAL would not formerly recognize the emergency 
regulation. 
Therefore, any lawful use of restricted funds is 
permissible at this time, except in connection with the support 
or opposition of candidates for elective office. 
Thank you for your advice. 
Sincerely, 
Peter A. Bagatelos 
PAB:fpi 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Peter A. Bagatelos 
Bagatelos & Fadem 
July 6, 1989 
The International Building 
601 California street, Suite 1801 
San Francisco, CA 
94108 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-395 
Dear Mr. Bagatelos: 
We received your letter requesting confirmation of advice 
under the political Reform Act on July 3, 1989. 
Your letter has 
been assigned to John Wallace for response. 
If you have any 
questions, you may contact him directly at (916) 322-5901. 
If the letter is appropriate for confirmation without further 
analysis, we will attempt to expedite our response. 
A confirming 
response will be released after it has gone through our approval 
process. 
If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the 
staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you 
shortly to advise you. 
In such cases, the normal analysis, review 
and approval process will be followed. 
You should be aware that your letter and our response are 
public records which may be disclosed to any interested person 
upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
KED:plh:confadv1 
Sincerely, 
{ 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916) 322;5660
